# Pi Extensions Reference

## Overview

Pi extensions live in `config/pi/extensions/` and are synced to `~/.pi/agent/extensions/` by `xtrm install pi` (interactive) or `runPiInstall()` (non-interactive, used by `xtrm install`).

## Directory Package Format

As of v0.5.0, each extension must be a **directory package** (not a flat `.ts` file).

Structure:
```
config/pi/extensions/<name>/
├── index.ts        # Extension implementation
└── package.json    # npm-style manifest required by Pi
```

`package.json` format:
```json
{
  "name": "@xtrm/pi-<name>",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "exports": { ".": "./index.ts" }
}
```

Pi will silently ignore extensions that lack `package.json` with an `exports` field.

## Extension Catalog

| Extension | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `auto-session-name` | Sets session name from git branch automatically |
| `auto-update` | Prompts to update Pi when a newer version is detected |
| `beads` | Beads (`bd`) issue tracking integration — claim/close/memory-gate |
| `bg-process` | Background process management |
| `compact-header` | Compact session header display |
| `custom-footer` | Status line with beads chip and session info |
| `custom-provider-qwen-cli` | Qwen CLI as a Pi provider |
| `git-checkpoint` | Automatic git checkpoints during sessions |
| `minimal-mode` | Reduced-verbosity session mode |
| `plan-mode` | Planning phase UI with beads integration |
| `quality-gates` | Quality gate enforcement hooks |
| `service-skills` | Service skill catalog activation |
| `session-flow` | Claim sync (notifies on bd update --claim), stop gate (blocks agent end with unclosed in_progress claim), xt end reminder when session ends in a worktree |
| `todo` | Todo tracking extension |
| `xtrm-loader` | xtrm config loading at session start |

Shared utilities live in `config/pi/extensions/core/` (not an extension — imported by others).

## Installation

```bash
# Interactive (prompts for API keys, writes config files)
xtrm install pi

# Non-interactive (extensions + packages only, used by xtrm install)
xtrm install
```

Both paths:
1. Copy `config/pi/extensions/` → `~/.pi/agent/extensions/` (overwrite)
2. Register each extension: `pi install -l ~/.pi/agent/extensions/<name>`
3. Install npm packages from `config/pi/install-schema.json`

## Drift Detection

```bash
xtrm install pi --check
```

Compares source (`config/pi/extensions/`) vs installed (`~/.pi/agent/extensions/`) directories.
Reports `missing`, `stale` (hash mismatch on `package.json + index.ts`), and `up-to-date` extensions.
Exits `1` if any drift detected — suitable as a CI gate.
